issue_title: Z3 bindings don't build on .NET Core 2 without a lot of manual migration
issue_body:
I've tried to build the Z3 bindings on macos 10.13 using the .NET Core SDK version 2.1.3, and run into a few problems that needed manual intervention.

(In case this is an XY problem, the reason I'm trying to do this is because I use Z3 (through `z3x86win`) in an F# project, and I'm trying to migrate it from Mono to .NET Core, without any success: the `Microsoft.Z3.dll` in `z3x86win` seems to work at first through the .NET Standard shims. but then tries to pull in `kernel32.dll` even if given a valid native z3 dylib.)

* `project.json` being the prerelease version of the .NET Core project file, one has to run `dotnet migrate` to get a .NET Core 1.x MSBuild.
* This MSBuild doesn't have version information---I'm not sure how to make cmake populate this, so I had to manually add it.
* Even so, .NET Core 2.x seems to provide code contracts now, so if I try to upgrade the MSBuild to 2.x I have to delete `DummyContracts.cs`.
* Using cmake/Ninja, nothing seems to copy the `build/src/api/dotnet/*.cs` files to somewhere the .NET Core builder can find them, causing lots of `CS0246` errors for missing native API bridging.  Copying these manually worked.  I seem to have got this working before, so maybe it's an issue with the build generator I'm using...?

For more detail, here are the steps I've taken to get a successful build (against branch `d86ef02`):

```
$ git clone https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3 z3-2
$ mkdir z3-2/build
$ cd z3-2/build
$ cmake -G 'Ninja' ..
$ ccmake .
[ set BUILD_DOTNET_BINDINGS to ON, configure twice and generate ]
$ ninja
[ build succeeds ]
$ cd ../src/api/dotnet/core
```

The .NET Core project file is in Preview 2 `project.json` format, needing a `dotnet migrate`:

```
$ dotnet migrate
[ migration succeeds ]
$ dotnet restore
[ restoration succeeds ]
$ dotnet build
/.../src/api/dotnet/Expr.cs(62,16): error CS0246: The type or namespace name 'Z3_lbool' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?) [/.../src/api/dotnet/core/core.csproj]
[ 20 more CS0246 follow ]
```

The `core.csproj` `dotnet migrate` generates looks like this:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.Net.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>netcoreapp1.0</TargetFramework>
    <DefineConstants>$(DefineConstants);DOTNET_CORE</DefineConstants>
    <DebugType>portable</DebugType>
    <AssemblyName>Microsoft.Z3</AssemblyName>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <PackageId>core</PackageId>
    <PackageTargetFallback>$(PackageTargetFallback);dnxcore50</PackageTargetFallback>
    <RuntimeFrameworkVersion>1.0.4</RuntimeFrameworkVersion>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="..\*.cs" Exclude="bin\**;obj\**;**\*.xproj;packages\**" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

The glob at the bottom doesn't include `Native.cs` etc., which are in `build\src\api\dotnet`.  Copying these over seems to fix the build, but I'm not sure why this step is needed.

To get a .NET Core 2.0 build with an accurate version, I `mv DummyContracts.cs DummyContracts.cs.old` and hacked the msbuild to be:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.Net.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>netcoreapp2.0</TargetFramework>
    <DefineConstants>$(DefineConstants);DOTNET_CORE</DefineConstants>
    <DebugType>portable</DebugType>
    <AssemblyName>Microsoft.Z3</AssemblyName>
    <AssemblyVersion>4.6.0.0</AssemblyVersion>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <PackageId>core</PackageId>
    <AssetTargetFallback>$(AssetTargetFallback);dnxcore50</AssetTargetFallback>
    <RuntimeFrameworkVersion>2.0.4</RuntimeFrameworkVersion>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="..\*.cs" Exclude="bin\**;obj\**;**\*.xproj;packages\**" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

`dotnet build` then works and gives me a `Microsoft.Z3.dll` I can slot in front of the packaged version to get Z3 working.

I figure this kind of build is quite niche at the moment, but it'd be nice to have a slightly smoother build process!

issue_title: Adaptive Computation Time layer?
issue_body:
hi, guys:
    Recently, I began to use the lasagne because it implements the SPPLayer. It is very convenient.
while when I use it to build new model, I found that it has not enough function sometimes, such as MaskLayer,RollLayer,GatherLayer and other complicated model like Adaptive Computation Time,Attention,Neural Turing Machines.
  I implement a simple Adaptive Computation Time layer. I dont know whether it is right. any one who can check it?

class ACTLayer(Layer):
    def __init__(self, incoming, output_num, act_step=5, threshold=0.1, backwards=False, only=True,
                 nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.tanh,
                 W=lasagne.init.Uniform(), U=lasagne.init.Uniform(), B=lasagne.init.Constant(0.),
                 GW=lasagne.init.Uniform(), GB=lasagne.init.Constant(0.),
                 **kwargs):
        super(ACTLayer, self).__init__(incoming, **kwargs)

        if isinstance(incoming, tuple):
            input_shape = incoming
        else:
            input_shape = incoming.output_shape

        self.W = self.add_param(W, (input_shape[-1],output_num))
        self.U = self.add_param(U, (output_num,output_num))
        self.B = self.add_param(B, (output_num,))

        self.GW = self.add_param(GW, (output_num,))
        self.GB = self.add_param(GB, (1,))

        self.nonlinearity = nonlinearity
        self.output_num = output_num
        self.threshold = threshold #epsilon
        self.act_step = act_step
        self.backwards = backwards
        self.only = only

    def get_output_for(self, input, **kwargs):
        def act(nt, hid, gate, prev, xt, w, u, b, gw, gb, threshold):
            state = T.dot(xt, w) + T.dot(hid, u) + b
            hid=self.nonlinearity(state)
            gate = T.nnet.sigmoid(T.sum(T.dot(hid, gw) + gb))

            # gate = T.switch(T.or_(prev - gate < threshold, nt<=0.0), prev, gate)
            gate = T.switch(prev - gate < threshold, prev, gate)
            gate = T.switch(nt<=0.0, prev, gate)
            # return (hid, gate, prev-gate), theano.scan_module.until(prev <= 0.0)
            return (hid, gate, prev-gate)

        def step(xt, hid, w, u, b, gw, gb, threshold):
            ([hid, w, s], _) = theano.scan(
                        fn = act,
                        sequences=[numpy.arange(self.act_step)[::-1]],
                        non_sequences=[xt, w, u, b, gw, gb, threshold],
                        outputs_info=[hid, 0.0, 1.0]
            )
            return T.dot(w, hid.dimshuffle(1, 0, *range(2, input.ndim)))
            # return T.dot(hid, w)

        def mask_step(xt, mask, hid, w, u, b, gw, gb, threshold):
            output = step(xt, hid, w, u, b, gw, gb, threshold)
            hid_out = T.switch(mask, output, hid)
            return hid_out

        print(input, type(input), input.shape)
        (nbatch, length, feature) = input.shape
        #(nbatch,length,feature)->(length,nbatch,feature)
        input = input.dimshuffle(1, 0, *range(2, input.ndim))
        (output, updates) = theano.scan(
            fn=step,
            sequences=[input],
            non_sequences=[self.W, self.U, self.B, self.GW, self.GB, self.threshold],
            outputs_info=[T.zeros((nbatch, self.output_num))],
            # outputs_info=[T.zeros_like(self.B)],
        )
        #(length,nbatch,output_num)->(nbatch,length,output_num)
        output = output.dimshuffle(1,0, *range(2, output.ndim))
        return output

    def get_output_shape_for(self, input_shape):
        return (input_shape[0], input_shape[1], self.output_num)

good wish!

issue_title: Split loading yaml files from Project, WorkspaceProject
Tool - provide API to set configuration
issue_body:
Tools could be smarter to provide API to configure the template. A good start to at least provide basic options. This might be useful for projects where a template needs custom configuration based on some inputs (like a different platform, different application). The current use would be to have a template per that need or know a logic for that template to change it.

Use case:
A template which would be generic but requires some degree of configuration. For example 10 of platforms use one option to reduce the size, lets call that option (reduce_size). To enable this option for them, if that's the target it would load generic template and pass it to tools configuration API. 

```
template # for example can be IAR project template, generic for all code base for a project
# now we got a target which requires reduce_size to be set in this template (it's disabled by default). enable it
IARconfiguration(template).set_reduce_size(True)
# another example, more difficult, where IAR has Language selection (C, C++.....), where a parameter would be one of the options
IARconfiguration(template).set_language('C++')
```

issue_title: CI/coverage: Associate form, pynwb and integration tests with a different flag
issue_body:
From @ajtritt 

> I was wondering if there was a way to figure out which new code needs unit tests by running just the unit tests.

It is indeed possible. See https://docs.codecov.io/docs/flags

I suggest we run the test driver three times:

1.  with `--form` , associating the coverage flag `form`

2.  with `--pynwb`, associating the coverage flag `pynwb`

3.  with `--integration`, associating the coverage flag `integration`

issue_title: handle surrogate pairs in character offsets

CodeMirror indices must be converted from UTF-16 units to actual character offsets:

> I think it makes the most sense for the protocol spec to be 'characters', so if CodeMirror indices are returning UTF-16 units, it ought to be the responsibility of the Jupyter javascript to deal with surrogate pairs and turn that into actual character offsets. - @minrk

See:

* https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/pull/2509
* https://github.com/jupyter/jupyter_client/issues/259

issue_title: Make `DueSwitch._dump_collector_summary` public?
issue_body:
I am very new to this library, so there may be good reasons to avoid this. However, from my perspective, it seems natural to have `DueSwitch._dump_collector_summary` as a public function (perhaps exposed as `DueSwitch.due_summary` or something) to make using duecredit with standalone jupyter notebooks a little more self-contained. I am thinking along the lines of:

    # near top of notebook
    import os
    os.environ['DUECREDIT_ENABLE'] = 'yes'

    # body of notebook
    import fancymodule as fm
    fm.do_stuff()

    # section at the end
    fm.due._dump_collector_summary()

This way one doesn't have to deal with external files keeping track of citations, possibly being added to by other notebooks which could possibly be undesirable.

issue_title: setup.py needs README.md, but it is not in the tar.gz package
issue_body:
The setup.py file depends on the presence of README.md, but that file is not included in the .tar.gz version of the package.

With v0.1.4, this problem now comes up because the tar.gz version is the only one available on PyPI, so pip picks it.

With v0.1.3, this problem existed as well, but did not come up when installing via `pip install click-spinner`, because a .whl version existed in addition on PyPI, and pip apparently prefers the .whl version over the .tar.gz version. I don't fully understand why this does not surface in the .whl version, but I suspect that pip does not execute the setup.py when installing a .whl archive.

issue_title: Fast accessURL on many Dirac files
issue_body:
`DiracFile.accessURL` is great, but way too slow (~8 s). I often have jobs with few hundred subjobs. If I call `.accessURL()` on the output file of each subjob, it will take O(hour) to get all the urls, which is unacceptable.

My solution to this was to make one Dirac call for many files:
```python
from GangaDirac.Lib.Utilities.DiracUtilities import execute
opts = '--Protocol xroot,root'
cmd = 'dirac-dms-lfn-accessURL {} {}'.format(','.join(lfns), opts)
output = execute(cmd, shell=True)
```

Unfortunately, this is broken in 6.1.22 (and 6.1.24), MWE:
```python
[22:42:41]
Ganga In [6]: from GangaDirac.Lib.Utilities.DiracUtilities import execute

[22:42:46]
Ganga In [7]: execute('dirac-dms-lfn-accessURL /lhcb/user/r/rmatev/2016_08/136797/136797530/ntuples.root', shell=True)
ERROR    Error: insecure string pickle
ERROR    Unknown/Unexpected ERROR!!
...
ValueError: insecure string pickle
```

I'd appreciate a lot any workarounds as well as a proper longer term solution.

issue_title: Fix GUID of Gaudi output files uploaded to SE for LHCb
issue_body:
If an output file (like a DST) is produced in LHCb outside the Grid environment, and subsequently upoloded to an SE, it gets assigned the wrong GUID and a subsequent Gaudi job can't read it. So the following workflow

1. Brunel job running on a batch system produce a DST
2. The output DST is declared a DiracFile and uploaded to an SE.
3. DaVinci job reading this DST directly from SE attempts to run.

At the moment, step 3 will fail unless the script `dirac-lhcb-fix-file-guid` has been executed. Looking inside the script, it sets the GUID at upload from the result of a call to `getRootFileGUID` from `LHCbDIRAC/Core/Utilities/File.py`. The solution is to insert this call for LHCb users before the `put` method is used in `DiracFile`.

issue_title: Old DiracFile not accessible
issue_body:

I think I've understood how this happens and the fix should be fairly quick and effective once I get a chance and it would be good to see in 6.3.1 so we can load old jobs properly (only in 6.3.x).

issue_title: add events for TERM, HUP and INT
issue_body:
we should provide a mechanism to register some code when the app receives an INT or TERM (stopping), or a HUP (reload asked) signal 

Use cases: cleanup the workers pool, restart all backend connections, reload the configs etc.

Dummy implementation:

```python
import signal

def close():
    config.registry.notify(AppClosing, async=False)
    sys.exit(0)

def reload():
     config.registry.notify(AppRestarting, async=False)

signal.signal(signal.SIGHUP, reload)
signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, close)
signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, close)

issue_title: Add a kinto init command
incorporate bandwidths into CWT wavelet names for families `cmor`, `shan`, `fbsp`
issue_body:
The `cmor`, `shan` and `fbsp` `ContinuousWavelet` objects have both a `bandwidth_frequency` and `center_frequency` attribute.  If a user calls the `cwt` routine in Matlab these must be specified in the name as follows:

e.g. `cmor1.5-1` gives a complex Morlet wavelet with `bandwidth_frequency` of 1.5 and `center_frequency` of 1.0.

To do the equivalent in PyWavelets one currently has to first create a wavelet and then manually modify the attributes:
```Python
w = pywt.ContinuousWavelet('cmor')
w.bandwidth_frequency=1.5
w.center_frequency=1
```

If the user passes just `'cmor'` to the `cwt` routine in Matlab an error would be raised as the bandwidths are not specified.  In PyWavelets, this currently does not raise an error and default values are set  (these appear to be equivalent to `'cmor1.0-0.5' in Matlab).

I propose to allow parsing the 2 bandwidth parameters from the Wavelet names as is done in Matlab.

I think it is also probably good to **require** that these be specified than choosing some undocumented default.  (i.e. raise an exception if just the blank wavelet family name such as `cmor` is passed into `cwt` or `ContinuousWavelet`)

The default setting has already caused some confusion as in #307

Headers such as `X-Crawlera-Profile`, `X-Crawlera-Debug` and `X-Crawlera-Cookies` are quite often used by Crawlera users. As such it could be easier if this plugin offered builtin support for them as spider attributes, such as:

```python
class FooSpider(scrapy.Spider):
    name = 'foo'
    crawlera_profile = 'mobile'
    crawlera_cookies_enabled = False
    ...
```

Without them, users always have to write their custom Crawlera middlewares or, even worse, pass the headers explicitly in each and every request issued by the spiders.

What do you think about adding support to such attributes in a spider level?

issue_title: Remove orphaned content units by type
issue_body:
https://github.com/PulpQE/pulp-smash/issues/134 asks for tests to be written that verify Pulp correctly handles requests to the orphan API calls. We can do that by writing tests that touch everything listed [here](http://pulp.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dev-guide/integration/rest-api/content/orphan.html).

One of the orphan API calls allows you to delete all orphans of a particular type by calling DELETE `/pulp/api/v2/content/orphans/{content_type_id}/{content_unit_id}/`. There's a wide range of content types, and we should write tests verifying that we can do this for each of the content types.

The corresponding Pulp Automation tests are here: https://github.com/RedHatQE/pulp-automation/blob/master/tests/general_tests/test_10_orphans.py

The content types include at least the following:

* [ ] rpm
* [ ] package group
* [ ] package category
* [ ] erratum
* [ ] distribution
* [ ] drpm
* [ ] srpm
* [ ] yum
* [ ] puppet module
* [ ] iso
* [ ] docker
* [ ] python package

issue_title: Required fails on ReferenceColumn
issue_body:
It seems like setting `'Required'` on a `ReferenceColumn` causes `db.sync()` to fail. Setting `'Required'` on, say, `StringColumn` works just fine.

Here's a small reproducible case:

```
import orb


class MyReferencedTable(orb.Table):

    id = orb.IdColumn()


class MyTable(orb.Table):

    id = orb.IdColumn()
    name = orb.StringColumn(flags={'Required'})  # This works fine.
    reference = orb.ReferenceColumn(reference='MyReferencedTable',
                                    flags={'Required'})  # This doesn't.


def main():
    db = orb.Database('SQLite', 'test_required.db')
    db.activate()
    db.sync()


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

It makes sense to me that the error would be thrown -- after all, we've defined a `NOT NULL` column without providing a default value. So, my question is why does it work with a `StringColumn` even though a default is not provided there. Thanks a bunch!

issue_title: Remover `issued` como un campo considerado para establecer la igualdad de dos datasets
issue_body:
@lucaslavandeira al principio habíamos considerado `issued` como un campo que debería identificar un dataset (junto con los otros) pero estamos viendo que el nodo federador no trae ese campo de metadata de los nodos originales, crea el suyo propio.

`issued` tiene el significado de *federado por primera vez* en el nodo central y de *publicado por primera vez* en el nodo original.

Deberíamos remover este campo de la función que establece la igualdad de dos datasets y cambiar los tests acorde. Cuando lo tengas porfa haceme un PR y yo subo a pypi una nueva versión con esto cambiado.

Gracias!!!

issue_title: Modificar `push_dataset_to_ckan()` para soportar catálogos con perfil de metadatos 1.0
issue_body:
**Contexto**

La implementación actual sólo soporta el perfil 1.1 porque depende críticamente del `distribution_identifier`. Muchos catálogos van a tardar en migrar hacia ese perfil, y tener identificador obligatorio de distribución.

**Propuesta**

Modificar `push_dataset_to_ckan()` para que, ante la ausencia de identifier a nivel de distribution, genere un identificador para las distribuciones según una regla que preserve la inmutabilidad de referencia _frente a la mayor cantidad de casos, aunque no pueda asegurar la inmutabilidad de referencia en todos ellos_.

Una propuesta posible: usar el índice posicional en el array `distribution` para conformar el id:

`distribution_identifier` = `{dataset_identifier}_{distribution_index}``

Así el identificador de las 4 distribuciones de un dataset con identifier `ign_1` sería:

+ `ign_1_0`
+ `ign_1_1`
+ `ign_1_2`
+ `ign_1_3`

issue_title: Diseñar e implementar una función de comparación de catálogos.
issue_body:
Todas las funciones actuales de `pydatajson.DataJson()` actúan sobre un catálogo a la vez.

A fines de identificar fácilmente cambios entre distintas versiones de un mismo catálogo, resultaría útil que exista una función que compare algunos aspectos básicos de dos catálogos (títulos de sus datasets, validez de los metadatos y cantidad de distribuciones por dataset, como mínimo).

En principio, la función tomaría dos catálogos, `base_catalog` y `compare_catalog`, de manera que lo que esté en `base` y no en `compare` se marque como "eliminado", y lo que esté en `compare` pero no en `base` se marque como agregado, _alla_ GitHub pull requests

issue_title: mnist.load_data
issue_body:
when using mnist.load_data(one_hot=True), you encounter an error:

TypeError: only integer scalar arrays can be converted to a scalar index

This can be solved by editing mnist.py file on line 33

`def _read32(bytestream):
    dt = numpy.dtype(numpy.uint32).newbyteorder('>')
    return numpy.frombuffer(bytestream.read(4), dtype=dt)`

While it should be: 

`def _read32(bytestream):
    dt = numpy.dtype(numpy.uint32).newbyteorder('>')
    return numpy.frombuffer(bytestream.read(4), dtype=dt)[0]`

because it made it an error to treat a single-element array as a scalar for the purposes of indexing.


source: [http://stackoverflow.com/questions/34010859/tensorflow-beginner-tutorial-read-data-sets-fails](url)

issue_title: I find the keyword argument `unspent` in OutputsEndpoint.get ambiguous
issue_body:
* bigchaindb-driver version: 0.2.1

From the [documentation](https://docs.bigchaindb.com/projects/py-driver/en/latest/libref.html?highlight=outputs#bigchaindb_driver.driver.OutputsEndpoint.get):
> unspent (bool) – Whether to get the unconsumed outputs only. Defaults to False, meaning that both consumed, and unconsumed outputs will be returned.

IMO a better keyword is `unspent_only`. My first interpretation of `unspent` set to `False` was: return me everything but unspents.

Another idea (that I think is more explicit) is to add a `unspent` method to the `OutputsEndpoint`.

Another thing, `transactions` has a method called `retrieve`, while `outputs` has a method called `get`, would it make sense in your opinion to unify and use only one term?

issue_title: Show an "unreleased version" banner in RTD latest docs
issue_body:
ReadTheDocs' "latest" version comes from master, which is (usually) an unreleased development version. Most readers would probably prefer "stable" (which is the most-recent release tag).

Should add a banner to the top of "latest" docs pages warning the user it's for unreleased code. Similar to what Django does in their [dev-version docs](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/dev/).

I *think* the best way to do this via JavaScript. (That's [how RTD handles](https://github.com/rtfd/readthedocs.org/blob/master/readthedocs/core/static-src/core/js/doc-embed/version-compare.js) inserting their own "you are not using the most up to date version" warning in older versions.)

issue_title: Simpler setuptools integration
issue_body:
`pybind11` could provide a subclass of `setuptools.Extension` with the defaults provided by https://github.com/pybind/python_example/blob/master/setup.py, i.e. that would automatically set `language` to `c++` and append the relevant `include_dirs`.

Extra points if it also allows lets one infer the module name from the path of the first source, similarly to Cython (http://docs.cython.org/en/latest/src/userguide/source_files_and_compilation.html#multiple-cython-files-in-a-package).

The idea would be to let one put the .cpp file in the same folder as where one expects the .so in the python package hierarchy, and write a `setup.py` as simple as
```
from pybind11 import Extension

setup(
    ...,
    ext_modules=[Extension("*", ["path/to/extmodule.cpp"])])
```
(I don't think it is necessary to subclass `build_ext`; instead the new Extension ctor could just extend the `extra_compile_args` as needed.)

Perhaps `py::array_t` objects can be created to contain more dimensions? I haven't found any documentation on that unfortunately.

issue_title: Make it easier to install pybind11 with CMake
issue_body:
When installing a Python module library which used pybind11 I got an error message similar to [this stackoverflow question][q] because the include paths become different after installing the binary.

As a header-only library I think if it would be better to either

1. mark the headers as private instead of public, or
1. change the value based on if it is used build-time or install-time:

Quoting the quote from [the SO answer][a]:
> Include directories usage requirements commonly differ between the build-tree and the install-tree. The BUILD_INTERFACE and INSTALL_INTERFACE generator expressions can be used to describe separate usage requirements based on the usage location. Relative paths are allowed within the INSTALL_INTERFACE expression and are interpreted relative to the installation prefix. For example:
>
>     target_include_directories(mylib PUBLIC
>       $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include/mylib>
>       $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:include/mylib>  # <prefix>/include/mylib
>     ) 

[q]: http://stackoverflow.com/q/25676277/245614
[a]: http://stackoverflow.com/a/25681179/245614

issue_title: Certbot always checks default cli.ini locations when the --config option is set
issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version):
```
[root@srv01 nginx]# cat /etc/redhat-release
Fedora release 26 (Twenty Six)
```

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):
OS Package manager
```
[root@srv01 nginx]# rpm -qi certbot
Name        : certbot
Version     : 0.19.0
Release     : 1.fc26
Architecture: noarch
Install Date: Wed 11 Oct 2017 06:09:51 AM CEST
Group       : Unspecified
Size        : 23213
License     : ASL 2.0
Signature   : RSA/SHA256, Sat 07 Oct 2017 01:54:44 AM CEST, Key ID 812a6b4b64dab85d
Source RPM  : certbot-0.19.0-1.fc26.src.rpm
Build Date  : Sat 07 Oct 2017 01:22:34 AM CEST
Build Host  : buildhw-03.phx2.fedoraproject.org
Relocations : (not relocatable)
Packager    : Fedora Project
Vendor      : Fedora Project
URL         : https://pypi.python.org/pypi/certbot
Summary     : A free, automated certificate authority client
```

## I ran this command and it produced this output:
````
[root@srv01 nginx]# certbot certificates -vvv --config "/etc/nginx/letsencrypt/cli.ini"
usage:
  certbot [SUBCOMMAND] [options] [-d DOMAIN] [-d DOMAIN] ...

Certbot can obtain and install HTTPS/TLS/SSL certificates.  By default,
it will attempt to use a web server both for obtaining and installing the
certificate.
certbot: error: unrecognized arguments: true
````

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:
Having explicitly set the config file for certbot to use, I would not expect it to also load an old config file in one of the default locations.

In my case, I was working on moving my Lets Encrypt config directory elsewhere and manually set the --config flag to load the new config file which also contains the config_dir directive in it which points to the new location. Despite this, certbot still appears to load the cli.ini from the default location (/etc/letsencrypt/cli.ini) despite explicitly setting both of these paths.

## Contents of the relevant cli.ini files
The `/etc/letsencrypt/cli.ini` contained errors in it which is what caused the error message. The issue here, however, is not with the error itself, but the fact that the file was parsed at all having explicitly set a different config file location which also set a different config_dir. To clarify, the cli.ini specified in the command line arguments does not contain any errors.

```
[root@srv01 nginx]# cat /etc/letsencrypt/cli.ini
config-dir = /etc/nginx/letsencrypt
email = ricky.grassmuck@gmail.com

rsa-key-size = 4096
authenticator = standalone
agree-tos = true

pre-hook = "docker stop nginx"
post-hook = "/bin/bash /etc/nginx/scripts/post-cert.sh"

allow-subset = true
expand = true
```
## Strace output showing certbot opening the files, it appears that it is set to check those paths first before the one specified via the command line switch.
```
[root@srv01 nginx]# grep "lstat\|open" certbot.strace | grep cli.ini
5362  lstat("/etc/letsencrypt/cli.ini", {st_dev=makedev(0, 40), st_ino=8147589, st_mode=S_IFREG|0644, st_nlink=1, st_uid=0, st_gid=0, st_blksize=4096, st_blocks=8, st_size=262, st_atime=1508648745 /* 2017-10-22T07:05:45.760294277+0200 */, st_atime_nsec=760294277, st_mtime=1508648285 /* 2017-10-22T06:58:05.878885688+0200 */, st_mtime_nsec=878885688, st_ctime=1508648285 /* 2017-10-22T06:58:05.878885688+0200 */, st_ctime_nsec=878885688}) = 0
5362  open("/etc/letsencrypt/cli.ini", O_RDONLY|O_CLOEXEC) = 4

5362  open("/etc/nginx/letsencrypt/cli.ini", O_RDONLY|O_CLOEXEC) = 5
```

issue_title: Get ACMEv2 support in Certbot
issue_body:
Step 2 of #5365 

#### Certbot core

- [x] Delete lines from `certbot.util.enforce_domain_sanity` that error on wildcards. We should still have code somewhere that errors if a wildcard is requested from a v1 endpoint because according to the spec "Wildcard domain names (with "*" as the first label) MUST NOT be included in authorization requests" and we should provide a clean error message to the user.
- [x] Improve error message if a wildcard is requested with an authenticator that can't do DNS challenges.
- [x] Don't make cert names with wildcards by default.
- [x] Fix `--allow-subset-of-names` for ACMEv2. This could be done in `certbot.client.obtain_certificate`, by having it repeatedly create a CSR, request a `new_order`, and perform authorizations until all authorizations pass. If any fail, we should create a new CSR with only the domains that succeeded. The order/authorizations would then be passed to `obtain_certificate_from_csr`.
- [x] Need to show link to TOS before calling `register`/`new_account` on the client from the acme module when ACMEv2 is used. Doing this requires being able to get TOS from ACMEv2 directory, ideally without making the user of the ACME library reach into the directory object themselves. We also need to skip this code for ACMEv1 and not try and show TOS again after registering for ACMEv2. **(Suggests acme change)**
- [x] Call `new_account`/`register` based on ACME version **(Could be mitigated by #5491 or changes to `acme.client.ClientV2`)**
- [x] If current design of #5374 stays, we have to make CSR before performing challenges in `certbot.client.obtain_certificate` because the CSR is needed earlier by the client in ACME. **(Could be mitigated by #5491 or changes to #5374)**
- [x] `certbot.auth_handler.get_authorizations` (or closely related function) should take a dict/list of authorization objects instead of domains. We can have another function on the `auth_handler` take domains and create authzs if we wanted for convenience of supporting ACMEv1 **(Could be mitigated by #5491 by keeping order object internal to acme client)**
- [x] We now get back the fullchain from the ACME server rather than separate leaf and chain certs. These need to be split (probably by just looking for the first line containing "END CERTIFICATE"). **(Could be mitigated by #5491)**

#### Apache and Nginx

- [x] Open question: Should Apache/Nginx suggest wildcards found in the config with `get_all_names`?
- [x]  Have Nginx and Apache installers support wildcards (deploy and enhance) in the case when there is one vhost matching the wildcard. Doing this involves making sure we can properly search the config for the vhost serving the wildcard (doing this 100% correctly could be hard/impossible for server names that are regexes) and fixing small things that break if wildcards are used. For example, `if ($host = *.example.com) {` doesn't make sense for Nginx redirects.
- [x] Possibly have Apache and Nginx installers (offer to) modify all vhosts that match the wildcard.

issue_title: dependency incompatibility with the latest google-cloud package

issue_title: Install requirements fail because of `pyliblzma` on MacOS
issue_body:
## Details

On MacOS, installing requirements with `pip install -r requirements/develop.pip` is failing to install `pyliblzma`:
```
In file included from src/liblzma.c:1:
  src/liblzma.h:24:10: fatal error: 'lzma.h' file not found
  #include <lzma.h>
           ^
  1 error generated.
  error: command 'clang' failed with exit status 1

  ----------------------------------------
  Failed building wheel for pyliblzma
  Running setup.py clean for pyliblzma
Failed to build pyliblzma
```

## Possible Fix

The fix on MacOS Sierra is (See this post on [StackOverflow](http://stackoverflow.com/a/37594849/6381715)):

```
brew install xz
```

issue_body:
Something like this for a startup:

    md = ColumnarModelData.from_data_frame(
        PATH,
        val_idxs,
        df,
        yl.astype(np.float32),
        cat_flds=categories,

You'll get this error:

It looks like the predict_array interface doesn't have a way to pass in categorical vs continuous variables, which is required for the underlying class MixedInputModel(nn.Module). In my read around, maybe the predict_array function needs to be overridden for StructuredLearner.

issue_title: 405-errors cause `freeze` to fail
issue_body:
As of v0.13 the crawler in the `_build_one`-function will cause the application to fail if it receives any response codes other than `200`, `404`, `301`, or `302`. I believe this may be intended to catch response errors in the 500-range because an unresponsive server shouldn't be processed, however applications with endpoints that allow `POST`-requests but not `GET`-requests will always fail. Likewise, `403`-errors would also cause the application to fail.

I believe it may be better to either explicitly catch errors in the 500-range, or add a configuration-option to ignore all 400-errors (like `FREEZER_IGNORE_404_NOT_FOUND`).

Fails with

AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)

----> 1 ray.put(1)

/Users/rkn/Workspace/ray/lib/python/ray/worker.py in put(value, worker)
   1274     The object ID assigned to this value.

-> 1276   with log_span("ray:put", worker=worker):
   1277     check_main_thread()
   1278     check_connected(worker)

This is simple to fix. We just need to move the `check_connected` call before the logging call in all the worker methods.

issue_title: Option to use hostnames instead of explicit ip addresses
issue_body:
Can we start ray using hostnames for the redis_address instead of an explicit IP?
This might be difficult to do correctly on the master node.
However, it should be easy on the worker node, and really only matters for the worker nodes connecting to the master.

Currently, plasma manager complains if a hostname is supplied instead of an IP.
The issue is [parse_ip_addr](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/master/src/plasma/plasma_manager.cc#L1749) is returning -1 since the following [regex](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/master/src/common/net.cc#L11) doesn't capture hostnames.

A simple quick fix I found was changing "%15[0-9.]:%5[0-9]" to "%[^:]:%5[0-9]".
There's probably a more careful way to do this using strtok.

issue_title: Add tips for development to documentation.
issue_body:
We should add some tips for development to the documentation. Some things that came up recently.

1. If you are experiencing a test failure, you can run an individual test as follows.

    ```
    python test/runtest.py APITest.testKeywordArgs
    ```

2. Note that usually only the first test failure matters. Once one test fails, often all subsequent tests fail. We should fix this.

3. You should install Ray with `python setup.py develop` (in `ray/python/`). That way, when you change the Python code, you won't need to reinstall Ray. However, if you change the C++ code, you will still need to recompile (this can be done with `./build.sh` (in `ray/`).

4. If you want to directly inspect the values in the *primary Redis shard*, you can query it with commands like

    ```python
    r = ray.worker.global_worker.redis_client
    r.keys("*")
    ```

issue_title: Task priorities
issue_body:
It seems this is currently not yet possible? It would be great if I could spawn multiple tasks and provide priority to them. Something like `fun.remote(priority=0.4)`. In that case if I spawn more tasks than the cluster can support, those with higher priority would start first. For our needs priority could be just a numeric number.

(And then after first tasks return, I could kill the rest if I see that I do not need them anymore.)

### Description

If you navigate over to https://streamlink.github.io/ you'll see that the titles I have on the donate page are showing up as links. I believe this issue is caused by having a toctree depth of 2 set in the index which is picking up the fact that I'm linking to people's profiles.

issue_title: Option to see at what time of the VOD streamlink is stopped/interrupted
issue_body:
### Checklist

- [] This is a bug report.
- [x] This is a feature request.
- [ ] This is a plugin (improvement) request.
- [x] I have read the contribution guidelines.

I use streamlink on my raspberry pi with VLC or Omxplayer. Both of these players on Raspberry don't have controls once the video starts (that means no video seeking).

Now that I use the experimental command "--hls-offset-start", I am finally able to start any VOD at a specified time. However, if the stream interrupts itself, or if I stop it, I have absolutely no idea of the time I have to specify to start again at the right time.

I don't think this is already possible with streamlink, but I think it could be really useful for this case, if there was an option that outputs the time of the VOD once streamlink stops.

Would it be a good idea ?

### Description

error: Media lookup error: You don't have permission to do this.
```

### Reproduction steps / Explicit stream URLs to test

Be in the US (either physically or through VPN) or pass a US session ID to streamlink and use one of the following URLs:

1. http://www.crunchyroll.com/nekomonogatari-black/episode-1-tsubasa-family-part-1-677667
2. http://www.crunchyroll.com/nekomonogatari-black/episode-2-tsubasa-family-part-2-677669

### Comments, screenshots, etc.

The show is geo blocked in my country (DE), so I have to use a US session ID and that worked with every other geoblocked Crunchyroll show so far.   
I've also tried using a US VPN but that didn't make a difference either.

I have tested other geoblocked and non geoblocked Crunchyroll shows under the same conditions and they worked without any issues.

The show does not require CR Premium and plays in both the Official app and through youtube-dl.

issue_title: [V3] Optional Embed system
issue_body:
### Type:
- [X] Suggestion
- [ ] Bug

Embeds don't play nice on Mobile, and they don't show up for anyone with link previews disabled. To stick all core features in embeds punishes mobile users with settings configured for conservative data usage.

Embeds on core commands whose functionality do not require link previews if not using an embed, should be displayable without the embed as well. (Notably, Steams shows picture of the stream from twitch, so this would be an example where not using an embed doesn't change things)

This would involve 3 additional settings in the bot config, possibly a flag in the CLI to start with embeds disabled (default enabled) and 3 commands in a command group for managing it, as well as restructuring the output flow of many core commands to conform to these settings. This would also likely involve injecting into context these settings and a property which would resolve to either true or false based on them and ctx.channel's type.

Settings: 
Global default: In the absence of a guild or user override, should embeds be used
Guild setting: Guild specific embed setting. This would apply in guild channels for anything other than the helpformatter.
User setting: DMs and help formatter would use this before falling back to the global default.

This also does not mean giving up formatting. As an example, Here is what the help could look like with YAML syntax highlighting
![screenshot from 2018-03-15 00-44-27](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22035876/37444793-0c0351da-27ea-11e8-9daa-a00d97fcf0d8.png)

Or the cogs:
![screenshot from 2018-03-15 00-53-23](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22035876/37444983-4c88e3cc-27eb-11e8-8bf8-8ffeb9813a9c.png)

issue_title: chained commands
issue_body:
It is common in Docker to chain commands together into a single layer and break the command so it does not exceed 80 characters. For instance:

```
RUN apt-get update -y && \
  apt-get-install -y --no-install-recommends \
     python \
     python3 && \
  rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* 
```

This is converted to Singularity as

```
%post
apt-get update -y && \
apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
python \
python3 && \
rm -rf /var/lig/apt/lists/*
```

The builder then interprets this as 5 effective commands rather than 1 long command split over 5 lines. The builder fails when trying to run `python`.

issue_title: High-level networking interface
issue_body:
We should have something that's just a smidge higher level than `trio.socket`. This is something that could go into an external library, but:

* I'd like to do TLS at this layer (so it's not bound to socket objects specifically), and in 2017 it really feels like TLS should be an included battery
* Getting started with a simple echo server shouldn't require boilerplate or third-party extensions
* I'm feeling the need for a basic stream abstraction in downstream libraries, and there are interoperability benefits to defining that in `trio` itself

The goal wouldn't be to compete with like, all of netty or twisted or something, but just to provide some minimal extensible abstractions for endpoints, connecting, listening. Maybe UDP too, though that can wait.

Things that should be easy:

* Connecting to a given hostname+port, with ipv4/ipv6 taken care of (ideally with happy-eyeballs)
* ...same as above, but with TLS enabled
* Making a simple server that listens on both ipv4 and ipv6
* Making a simple server that listens on a unix domain port

Maybe:
* Plugging in some code to handle systemd socket activation
* Plugging in some code to do something sensible with the [haproxy PROXY protocol](http://www.haproxy.org/download/1.5/doc/proxy-protocol.txt)

A source of inspiration is twisted's "endpoints": [tutorial](https://twistedmatrix.com/documents/current/core/howto/endpoints.html), [list of client endpoints](https://twistedmatrix.com/documents/current/api/twisted.internet.interfaces.IStreamClientEndpoint.html), [list of server endpoints](https://twistedmatrix.com/documents/current/api/twisted.internet.interfaces.IStreamServerEndpoint.html), [a much fancier server endpoint](https://txacme.readthedocs.io/en/stable/). I'm not a big fan of the plugins + string parsing thing, just because of the whole injecting-arbitrary-code-into-people's-processes thing that plugins do ... though I see the advantage when it comes to exposing this stuff as part of a public interface and wanting it to be extensible there.

We probably want:

* A basic Stream API. There's a sketch in `trio._streams`. This is useful in its own right; the idea would be to implement TLS as a stream wrapper e.g., and we can use a `SendStream` and `RecvStream` for subprocess pipes, etc.

* Some sort of connect and listen factories for streams.

Lots more TBD; for now this is a placeholder to think more about.

Related: #9 (tls support), #13 (more ergonomic server quick-start), #14 (batched accept), #72 (socket option defaults – some might make more sense to set as part of the high-level interface)

issue_labels: [{'id': 523592775, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/python-trio/trio/labels/missing%20piece', 'name': 'missing piece', 'color': 'fbca04', 'default': False}, {'id': 523633446, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/python-trio/trio/labels/todo%20soon', 'name': 'todo soon', 'color': 'd4c5f9', 'default': False}, {'id': 537057261, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/python-trio/trio/labels/user%20happiness', 'name': 'user happiness', 'color': '0e8a16', 'default': False}]

Can keep it pretty minimal because people all have their own ideas of how things should work, but I think there's enough little things that it could help with to be worthwhile:

- sphinxcontrib-trio set up

- CI setup similar to trio's own (in particular linux/mac/windows + pypy testing is not trivial... maybe not everyone wants it, and you can always turn it off, but I bet a lot of people would appreciate it)

- pytest-trio set up (well, need to extract it first... #27)

- a `~=` dependency on trio

- `python_requires`

I've never actually done anything with cookiecutter so no idea how difficult this will be, but it doesn't look too bad.

If anyone's looking to contribute but doesn't feel ready to dive into coroutine-land, then this would be a good place.

issue_title: Why is stack info in findCaller limited to PY3?
issue_body:
In ABSLLogger.findCaller(), the documentation says:
```
stack_info: bool, when using Python 3 and True, include the stack trace as the fourth item returned instead of None.
```
https://github.com/abseil/abseil-py/blob/master/absl/logging/__init__.py#L885

First off, that's a bit confusing. It makes it sounds like it'll return None if you're using PY2, but it actually returns 3-tuple, making PY2-3 compatibility messy.

The bigger question though is why? I'm not sure what part of that code isn't compatible. Python 2 has StringIO and traceback.print_stack(), so why is stack information only done in PY3?

issue_title: Allow template type per template
issue_body:
Currently `template_type` can only appear at:

```yaml
configuration:
  template_type: ...
  ...
```

While that default is ok, it should be possible to override it per template.
e.g.
```
targets:
  - output: LICENSE
    template: license.mako
    template_type: mako
```

    pip3 install --upgrade pip3 h5py pillow
    pip3 install --upgrade numpy tensorflow-gpu
    git clone https://github.com/studioml/studio.git /opt/studio

issue_title: warn on bad matlab style
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **Mark Mikofski (Bitbucket: [bwanamarko](https://bitbucket.org/bwanamarko), GitHub: Unknown)**

----------------------------------------

1. used builtin as name, _EG:_ (Token.Name.Builtin, 'gamma')
2. bad indentation
3. arrays spread over multiple lines without continuation dots
4. semicolons in properties
5. tabs instead of spaces
6. filename function/classdef mismatch
7. spaces before signature
8. classdef docstring not indented
9. empty properties or methods block
10. function with no args

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/bwanamarko/sphinxcontrib-matlabdomain/issue/13

* IOC Fanger version: v1.0.0
* Python version: 3.6.4

### Description

```python
ioc_fanger.defang('8.8.8.8') == '8[.]8.8[.]8'
ioc_fanger.defang('a.b.c') == 'a[.]b.c'
```

The fact the middle dot was not escaped took me by surprise and seem like a bug or is there an explanation for this?

Seems to me like every other defanger just replaces every `.` with `[.]`, so I would expect `ioc_fanger.defang`do the same.

issue_title: MacOS testing on Travis is very slow
issue_body:
There are generally quite large queues on the [status page](https://www.traviscistatus.com) for MacOS. We also are only able to test the latest version of Python, as it is quite difficult to get a consistent version of Python (except by using MacPython, which I feel is not ideal).

So where Linux builds on Travis finish in ~3 minutes (because parallel), and those on AppVeyor take about 3 minutes each (so 12 minutes total), the test for MacOS can easily take an hour to even start.

I really want to test for all major platforms (which is why I added it), but with these runtimes I feel it's not worth it. Maybe we can enable it again when a better service becomes available, e.g. when Apple starts allowing/supporting MacOS to run on non-Apple hardware (likely never).

issue_title: Add flexibility to agent loading so agents can be stored outside RLBot folder / so bot makers can use gradle dependency on framework
issue_body:
Currently agent py files need to be placed somewhere inside the RLBot folder, otherwise # Path to module from runner won't work

I don't like that situation because I want to put my bot in its own repository outside the RLBot folder, and not need to copy files over

I think @DomNomNom (NomBot) achieved that flexibility once, then it was broken, now I want to bring it back again. Dom's commit: https://github.com/RLBot/RLBot/commit/10e1e0612ba30c5cdcf691e4e3ff7a40414858f2

One of my constraints will be that v1 and v2 of a python bot should be easy to fight against each other without part of their imports conflicting

From Marvin:
About the pathing issue not sure if you're aware but it's not necessary to add the folder to sys path, you could use importlib spec to import from a path
You can check this commit for reference
https://github.com/RLMarvin/RLBot/commit/2ad7230a53ad1745ec0ed19b8f22e70c33df8385
I was using two paths one for the agent and one for the cfg but if you assume they are in the same directory you can simply use one
This will make it possible to have the agent outside runner.py folder and fix imports with multiple agents having the same name
